metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend AC5's grep pattern (or add a second grep) to also catch 'NOT repo-wide' references alongside 'release\.yml only', ensuring all comment variants described in Scope are verified.
evidence: Scope: 'Update the actionlint-scope comments (lines ~19, 97, 101, 107, 115) that say "release.yml only"/"NOT repo-wide"'
AC5 grep: `grep -rniE 'actionlint[^.]*scoped to .?release\.yml|actionlint[^.]*release\.yml only' Makefile docs/` — does not contain 'NOT repo-wide' as a search term
findings.finding: AC5's verification grep does not cover all stale comment text described in Scope. Scope explicitly calls for updating comments that say 'NOT repo-wide' (in addition to 'release.yml only'), but AC5's grep pattern only matches 'actionlint[^.]*scoped to .?release\.yml' and 'actionlint[^.]*release\.yml only' — a comment revised to still say 'NOT repo-wide' (in a new form) would pass the grep while the described update was incomplete.
findings.location: Scope section (comment lines ~19, 97, 101, 107, 115) vs AC5 grep pattern
scenarios: A comment is reworded to still say 'actionlint NOT repo-wide' (without 'release.yml only'), the AC5 grep returns nothing, but the comment still describes the old behavior incorrectly.
findings.suggested_fix: Add `| grep -rniE 'actionlint[^.]*not repo.wide' Makefile docs/` as a second proof step in AC5, or broaden the pattern to `'actionlint[^.]*(scoped to .?release\.yml|release\.yml only|not repo.wide)'`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Extend the AC5 verification grep (or the Scope edit list) to also catch `release.yml actionlint` / `release.yml.*actionlint` patterns so the macOS skip-notice on Makefile line 115 is explicitly updated and the AC self-proof actually verifies it.
evidence: Makefile line 115: `echo "lint: actionlint unavailable on $$(uname -s); skipping release.yml actionlint audit (gated on the Linux CI leg)"`
AC5 grep pattern: `actionlint[^.]*scoped to .?release\.yml|actionlint[^.]*release\.yml only` — neither arm matches `release.yml actionlint` (reversed order)
Plan Scope section says update comments lines ~19, 97, 101, 107, 115 — line 115 skip-echo is listed but the AC grep wouldn't validate it was actually updated
findings.finding: The AC5 self-proof grep (`grep -rniE 'actionlint[^.]*scoped to .?release\.yml|actionlint[^.]*release\.yml only' Makefile docs/`) has a blind spot: Makefile line 115 contains the stale phrase 'skipping release.yml actionlint audit (gated on the Linux CI leg)' where 'release.yml' precedes 'actionlint', so neither regex arm matches it. After the widening change this skip-notice will still say 'release.yml actionlint audit', misleading readers into thinking only release.yml is audited on Linux — yet the AC's own verification command would pass, falsely certifying no stale wording remains.
scenarios: Developer runs the AC5 verification grep after the change, gets zero results (passes), but line 115 still prints 'skipping release.yml actionlint audit' in CI output when actionlint is unavailable on macOS — permanently misleading.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a second grep pass in AC5: `grep -rniE 'release\.yml[^.]*actionlint' Makefile docs/` (catches reversed-order phrasing), or explicitly list line 115's skip echo as requiring an update to say 'skipping all-workflows actionlint audit'.

evidence: verify command `grep -rniE 'actionlint[^.]*scoped to .?release\.yml|actionlint[^.]*release\.yml only' Makefile docs/` inspects output with `grep` but asserts nothing — add an exit-code or comparison (-q, [ "$(...)" -eq N ], jq -e) so a failure is observable
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Tighten AC3 to name a specific previously-unguarded workflow (e.g. `reconcile-bridge.yml` or its canary) as the mutation target, e.g. 'inject `${{ runner.temp }}` into a job-level env in `.github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml`, run `make lint` → non-zero exit, then `git checkout`.
evidence: "Proof: add the line, run `make lint` → non-zero exit citing `runner`, then `git checkout` to revert" — no target workflow is named; 'any workflow' is implied but not stated.
The entire motivation for the change is that non-release.yml workflows were unguarded; the mutation test is only meaningful when applied to a file that was previously unguarded.
findings.finding: AC3's mutation-test proof command does not specify WHICH non-release.yml workflow to inject the bad `${{ runner.temp }}` env into, leaving the executor to guess; using release.yml itself would satisfy the letter of the AC without proving the newly widened coverage.
scenarios: An executor adds the bad line to release.yml (already covered before this change), confirms `make lint` fails, and records the proof — without ever validating that the newly covered workflows are actually caught.
findings.suggested_fix: AC3 proof: 'Inject `  TEMP_DIR: ${{ runner.temp }}` under a job-level `env:` block in `.github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml`; run `make lint`; confirm non-zero exit with `runner` in the error output; run `git checkout .github/workflows/reconcile-bridge.yml` to revert.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an automated failure-path test to `test_ci_actionlint_scope.py` (or a new fixture) that injects a `${{ runner.temp }}` job-level `env:` into a temporary workflow file and asserts `make lint` (or a direct `actionlint` subprocess call) exits non-zero — so the guard covers the actual error class, not just Makefile text.
evidence: AC3: 'Reintroducing a ${{ runner.temp }} job-level env into any workflow makes make lint FAIL. Proof: add the line, run make lint → non-zero exit citing runner, then git checkout to revert.' — this is manual, not automated
AC4: 'assert the Makefile actionlints all workflows and fails if re-scoped to release.yml only' — this is a structural Makefile grep, not a behavioral execution test
The new test can pass even if actionlint is invoked with --no-color and runner-context checks are suppressed, because it only checks command text, not actual lint output
findings.finding: The automated test (test_ci_actionlint_scope.py) is purely structural — it greps Makefile text to assert actionlint is not scoped to $(RELEASE_WORKFLOW). The core failure path — that a bad-context expression in a non-release workflow actually causes `make lint` to exit non-zero — is only a manual proof step (AC3). No automated test exercises the real failure mode: introduce an invalid workflow → lint fails. This means the regression guard passes even if actionlint's runner-context check is misconfigured, disabled, or silently broken.
findings.location: AC3 (manual proof); AC4 / tests/unit/test_ci_actionlint_scope.py (automated test scope)
scenarios: actionlint is updated/reconfigured to suppress runner-context errors — structural test still passes, real bug would merge
A future refactor changes actionlint flags to disable the specific check — test stays green while the guard is broken
The whole purpose of the change is blocking the exact failure that caused the 2-day outage; having no automated proof of that failure path is a meaningful regression risk
findings.suggested_fix: In test_ci_actionlint_scope.py, add a test that: (1) writes a minimal workflow YAML with `${{ runner.temp }}` in a job-level `env:` to a temp file, (2) invokes `actionlint <tmpfile>` as a subprocess, (3) asserts returncode != 0. This directly verifies the error-detection behavior without requiring full `make lint` execution and can run offline/in-process.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Verify the 'zero cost' premise by documenting proof that `actionlint .github/workflows/*.yml .github/workflows/*.yaml` (or `actionlint` with auto-discovery) exits 0 on the current HEAD before implementing the Makefile change — e.g. paste the actual command output or CI run link into the plan.
evidence: Plan text: 'Cost is zero today: `actionlint .github/workflows/*.yml .github/workflows/*.yaml` already exits 0 with 0 findings (post-8002-fix).'

14 workflow files found in .github/workflows/ (codeql.yml, eval.yml, external-integration.yml, lockdown.yml, mirror-guard.yml, terraform-drift.yml, etc.) — none individually probed for actionlint-clean status by any repo file tool.
AC2 states 'make lint passes on the current tree. Proof: make lint exits 0.' — this AC is only passable if the runtime assertion is true.
Tools cannot run actionlint, so this assertion is unverifiable from repo file tools alone; fail-closed under G-7a.
findings.finding: The plan asserts 'Cost is zero today: `actionlint .github/workflows/*.yml .github/workflows/*.yaml` already exits 0 with 0 findings (post-8002-fix)' — a confident runtime claim that the current repo-wide workflow tree is clean under actionlint. This assertion is load-bearing for AC2 ('make lint passes on the current tree') and for the framing that the change is purely a scope expansion with no cleanup burden. It is unverifiable from repo file tools: the 14 workflows in .github/workflows/ (including codeql.yml, eval.yml, external-integration.yml, lockdown.yml, mirror-guard.yml, terraform-drift.yml, etc.) have not been probed for actionlint-clean status. If any of these workflows has a latent actionlint error, widening the gate immediately breaks make lint on the current tree — contradicting AC2 and the plan's 'zero cost' premise.
findings.location: What section: 'Cost is zero today: `actionlint .github/workflows/*.yml .github/workflows/*.yaml` already exits 0 with 0 findings (post-8002-fix)'
scenarios: If any of the 13 non-release workflows has a latent actionlint error (e.g. a bad context expression, undefined variable, or unknown action), widening make lint immediately breaks the CI gate on the current tree — the plan's 'zero cost' premise is wrong and remediation work is needed before the AC can pass.
The implementer widens the Makefile target, pushes, and the gerrit-verify gate immediately turns red on unrelated workflows — causing confusion and emergency revert.
findings.suggested_fix: Add a 'Pre-condition verification' note to the plan: 'Ran `actionlint .github/workflows/*.yml .github/workflows/*.yaml` on HEAD <SHA> — exit 0, 0 findings. (Output: <paste>).' If findings exist, enumerate which workflows need pre-fixes and add them as a prerequisite step.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify (in the plan or a Scope note) that the pre-commit `lint` hook is scoped to `types_or: [python, pyi]` and won't fire on workflow-only commits — either accept this as a known gap (CI is the real gate) or add `yaml` to the hook's `types_or` so the local gate also covers workflow changes.
evidence: `.pre-commit-config.yaml` lines 41-42: `pass_filenames: false` + `types_or: [python, pyi]` — hook only runs when Python files are staged
Plan 'How it is guarded': 'widening the Makefile target covers both legs automatically' — ambiguously implies the pre-commit hook is also covered
The pre-commit hook entry runs `make lint` but pre-commit only invokes it when files matching `types_or: [python, pyi]` are staged; a pure `.github/workflows/` commit skips it
findings.finding: The pre-commit `lint` hook is scoped to `types_or: [python, pyi]` and will NOT fire when a developer commits only a `.github/workflows/*.yml` change — so the local commit-gate remains blind to workflow-only edits even after this plan is implemented. The plan claims the widening 'covers both legs automatically' but that phrase refers only to the two CI legs (gerrit-verify + test.yml); it does not cover the pre-commit path for workflow-only commits. A developer could commit a malformed workflow, have the pre-commit hook silently skip `make lint`, and only be caught at the Gerrit Verified gate — which is fine for CI correctness but contradicts the implicit 'local gate also hardens' framing.
findings.location: .pre-commit-config.yaml (lint hook, `types_or: [python, pyi]`) and plan section 'How it is guarded'
scenarios: Developer edits only reconcile-bridge.yml locally, stages and commits — pre-commit hook does NOT run `make lint`, so the malformed workflow is not caught until the Gerrit Verified CI leg.
Plan description implies three guard layers (pre-commit, gerrit-verify, test.yml) but the first layer has a YAML-type blind spot.
findings.suggested_fix: In `.pre-commit-config.yaml`, change the `lint` hook's `types_or: [python, pyi]` to `types_or: [python, pyi, yaml]`, or add `files: '\.github/workflows/'` as a second trigger pattern. If the local gap is accepted as-is, add a note in the plan's 'How it is guarded' section explicitly stating 'the pre-commit hook only triggers on Python file changes; workflow-only commits rely on the Gerrit Verified gate exclusively'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the invocation form ambiguity: confirm whether `actionlint` (no-arg auto-discovery) or `actionlint .github/workflows/*.yml .github/workflows/*.yaml` (explicit globs) is the intended implementation, verify both are supported by actionlint v1.7.12, and address the Make shell glob-expansion risk for `*.yaml` when no `.yaml`-suffixed files exist (e.g., use `find` or no-arg form, or set nullglob).
evidence: Plan 'What': 'actionlint .github/workflows/*.yml .github/workflows/*.yaml already exits 0 with 0 findings'
Plan 'What': 'actionlint auto-discovers .github/workflows when given no path arg'
Plan 'Scope': 'invoke actionlint over ALL workflows (actionlint auto-discovers .github/workflows when given no path arg)'

The repo has 15 workflow files; a grep of .github/workflows shows no *.yaml files (all are *.yml except gerrit-verify.yaml and verify-identity.yaml) — meaning the *.yaml glob may behave differently depending on shell globbing settings in Make
The plan proposes two different invocation forms without specifying which one to implement or confirming they are interchangeable for actionlint v1.7.12
findings.finding: The plan asserts that `actionlint` auto-discovers `.github/workflows` when given no path argument, but the actual Scope section proposes `actionlint .github/workflows/*.yml .github/workflows/*.yaml` as the invocation. These two forms are not necessarily equivalent, and shell glob expansion of `*.yaml` may fail (expand to a literal `*.yaml` string) if no `.yaml`-suffixed workflow files exist, causing an unexpected actionlint error. The plan leaves the invocation form unresolved and does not confirm that both forms are supported by actionlint v1.7.12.
findings.location: What section ('actionlint auto-discovers .github/workflows when given no path arg') and Scope section ('actionlint .github/workflows/*.yml .github/workflows/*.yaml')
scenarios: Implementer uses no-arg auto-discovery form; actionlint picks up workflows differently than the explicit glob (e.g., if actionlint.yml config overrides discovery paths)
Shell glob `*.yaml` expands to a literal string `*.yaml` in a Make shell with nullglob off (the default), causing actionlint to error on a non-existent file argument and break `make lint`
The two invocation forms produce different exit codes or error messages on the same bad workflow, making the AC3 proof script ambiguous
findings.suggested_fix: Use the no-arg form (`actionlint`) in the Makefile, which is actionlint's own recommended way to scan all workflows and avoids shell glob pitfalls. Update the Scope and What sections to consistently name this form. Verify actionlint v1.7.12 release notes confirm no-arg auto-discovery of `.github/workflows`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm the exact actionlint invocation to use in the Makefile by running `actionlint --help` (or checking https://github.com/rhysd/actionlint docs) to verify: (a) whether no-arg mode auto-discovers `.github/workflows` from any working directory, OR (b) that the explicit glob form handles a zero-match `*.yaml` glob safely — and align the plan's 'What' and 'Scope' sections to describe a single, confirmed invocation.
evidence: What: 'actionlint .github/workflows/*.yml .github/workflows/*.yaml already exits 0 with 0 findings (post-8002-fix)'
Scope: 'invoke actionlint over ALL workflows (actionlint auto-discovers .github/workflows when given no path arg)'
Evidence class: Unverified — no --help output or documentation citation confirms the no-arg auto-discovery behavior from an arbitrary working directory
The existing Makefile (lines 111–113) passes a single explicit file path; neither invocation mode has been tested in the widened form against the Makefile shell context
findings.finding: The plan asserts two conflicting actionlint invocation modes without specifying which will be used in the Makefile: the 'What' section describes explicit glob args (`actionlint .github/workflows/*.yml .github/workflows/*.yaml`), while the 'Scope' section asserts `actionlint auto-discovers .github/workflows when given no path arg`. The no-arg auto-discovery behavior is an external CLI capability that is Unverified — it is stated as fact but not confirmed against `actionlint --help` or documentation. If auto-discovery does not traverse the nearest `.github/workflows` directory when invoked from the repo root (e.g., it requires being run from within `.github/`), the widened coverage would silently fail.
findings.location: What section ('actionlint .github/workflows/*.yml .github/workflows/*.yaml already exits 0'); Scope section ('actionlint auto-discovers .github/workflows when given no path arg')
scenarios: Developer runs `make lint` from the repo root; if actionlint's no-arg mode scans the CWD rather than recursing into `.github/workflows`, it finds no workflow files and exits 0 silently — the widened coverage is a no-op.

The explicit-glob approach is used instead, but `.github/workflows/*.yaml` expands to a literal string (no nullglob) since all 15 current workflows use `.yml`, causing actionlint to error on a non-existent path argument or silently skip it.
findings.suggested_fix: Use `actionlint .github/workflows/` (directory argument) or `actionlint` with no args if confirmed to auto-discover from repo root; remove the `*.yaml` glob arm if no `.yaml` files exist in the workflows directory. Verify with `actionlint --help | grep -A2 'Usage'` before committing to either approach.

findings.finding: The plan asserts that `tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py` enforces parity of the widened `make lint` gate, but the existing parity test only checks that the string `make lint` appears in both workflows (`_SHARED_GATE_SIGNATURES: 'lint (ruff)': 'make lint'`). After the proposed change, `make lint` still appears unchanged in both workflows — the parity test will pass whether or not the Makefile glob was correctly widened. A future regression narrowing the glob back to `RELEASE_WORKFLOW` would not be caught by this parity test.
findings.location: Scope section: 'Confirm test.yml + gerrit-verify.yaml both run the widened make lint (parity — see tests/unit/test_ci_workflow_parity.py)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add a unit test that reads `Makefile`, locates the actionlint invocation line(s), and asserts that the argument is NOT `$(RELEASE_WORKFLOW)` (or equivalently, asserts that a wildcard glob or `ALL_WORKFLOWS`-style variable is used). This test would have caught the original `f2bde8d3b5` regression and will catch any future narrowing.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Replace the AC1 proof command with one that machine-asserts the absence of RELEASE_WORKFLOW restriction, e.g. `sed -n '/^lint:/,/^actionlint-bin:/p' Makefile | grep 'actionlint' | grep -vq 'RELEASE_WORKFLOW'` (exits non-zero if the restriction is present or if no actionlint invocation is found).
evidence: "Proof: `sed -n '/^lint:/,/^actionlint-bin:/p' Makefile` shows the actionlint invocation is not restricted to `$(RELEASE_WORKFLOW)`" — the sed output requires a human to read and interpret; it does not exit non-zero if RELEASE_WORKFLOW still appears.
findings.finding: AC1's proof command prints the Makefile section but does not programmatically ASSERT the absence of RELEASE_WORKFLOW restriction; a visual-inspection-only command can pass silently even when the criterion is not met.
scenarios: An executor runs the sed command, sees a wall of Makefile text, judges it visually, and signs off — even if the RELEASE_WORKFLOW restriction is still present — because there is no machine-checkable assertion.
findings.suggested_fix: `sed -n '/^lint:/,/^actionlint-bin:/p' Makefile | grep 'actionlint' | grep -vq 'RELEASE_WORKFLOW' && echo PASS || echo FAIL`

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the preferred actionlint invocation form (no-arg auto-discovery or explicit glob) in the Scope section and ensure the AC1 proof command reflects the chosen form.
evidence: What section: '`actionlint .github/workflows/*.yml .github/workflows/*.yaml` already exits 0 with 0 findings'
Scope section: 'invoke actionlint over ALL workflows (actionlint auto-discovers `.github/workflows` when given no path arg)'
These two descriptions prescribe different Makefile invocations (no-arg vs. explicit glob).
findings.finding: The plan describes two mutually inconsistent implementation approaches for the actionlint invocation — no-arg auto-discovery ('actionlint auto-discovers `.github/workflows` when given no path arg') and an explicit double-glob ('actionlint .github/workflows/*.yml .github/workflows/*.yaml') — without declaring which one to implement.
findings.location: What section ('actionlint .github/workflows/*.yml .github/workflows/*.yaml already exits 0'); Scope section ('actionlint auto-discovers `.github/workflows` when given no path arg')
scenarios: An executor implementing the no-arg form may later find the AC1 sed proof command ambiguous (the no-arg invocation doesn't mention any path, making it harder to grep-assert coverage); an executor using the explicit glob may later add a workflow in a non-standard subdirectory that the glob misses.
findings.suggested_fix: Prefer no-arg: 'invoke `actionlint` (no path argument) so it auto-discovers `.github/workflows/`; remove the `$(RELEASE_WORKFLOW)` argument.' Update the AC1 proof command to assert the absence of any path argument: `sed -n '/^lint:/,/^actionlint-bin:/p' Makefile | grep 'actionlint' | grep -vqE 'RELEASE_WORKFLOW|\.github/workflows/[^ ]'`.

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
